characterCraft.note: At 160x360 the hunter reads as a built character with cap, face, coat, articulated gait, backpack, hose and distinct sweep, vacuum, brace and spook poses. Wisp, rascal, luggage-bearing bellhop and hatted concierge have separate silhouettes and reactions at the same small scale as the reference heroes.

environmentCraft.note: The cutaway hotel is continuously built across the strip: masonry facade and entrance, elevator spine, vents, cornices, floors, sconces and twenty-four authored furniture silhouettes create more architectural density than MACHINE HUNT and comparable material specificity to BLOCK MINE.

levelVariety.note: Gilded lobby arches and chandelier, moon-suite windows and bedroom props, spa tile and pipe networks, and ballroom curtains, night windows and instruments change composition and landmarks as well as palette. The stacked view keeps prior warm floors visible as permanent progression.

animationImpact.note: The inspected sequence shows a moving sweep arc, furniture tell, spectral reveal, stretched vent flight, suction beam, broken-plan reaction, bellhop entrance, concierge confrontation and broad room relight. Actor-minus-clean-plate evidence confirms gait and ghost motion without background pixels, while the apex burst confirms follow-through rather than static icon swaps.

readability.note: The truthful interactive footprint includes the active cast plus every visibly possessed prop on preview floors and remains under seventeen percent even in the boss frame; furniture stays below the 24px structure cap and every flee path has over 55 percent runway. Cyan dotted order, numbered targets, vent wards, possession eyes and the plain-language intent panel make containment forecastable at native size.

artDirectionCohesion.note: Spectral cyan and lilac consistently denote haunting while brass and cream denote safety and relight; burgundy, blue, teal and violet wings remain part of one nocturnal hotel. Character accents, furniture materials, plan marks, HUD frames and payoff light share the same crisp pixel language.
